name: comparative-analysis
description: Compare two or more tools, libraries, or approaches with structured pros/cons, tradeoffs, and a context-aware recommendation.

items.description: Comma-separated items to compare (e.g., 'React, Vue, Svelte')

use_case.description: Specific use case or context for the comparison

priorities.description: What matters most (e.g., 'performance, DX, ecosystem, learning-curve')

steps.template:
Compare: {{items}}
Use case: {{use_case}}
Priorities: {{priorities}}

Step 1 — Profile each option:
For each item:
- What it is (one sentence)
- Key strengths (top 3)
- Key weaknesses (top 3)
- Best suited for: [scenarios]
- Not ideal for: [scenarios]
- Maturity: [established/growing/emerging]
- Community: [size, activity, support quality]

Step 2 — Head-to-head comparison:
| Criterion | {{items}} |

Criteria to evaluate:
- Performance (benchmarks if available)
- Developer experience (tooling, docs, debugging)
- Ecosystem (libraries, plugins, integrations)
- Learning curve (time to productivity)
- Scalability (grows with project complexity)
- Maintenance (long-term cost, breaking changes)
- Community (hiring, support, longevity)

Score each 1-10 with brief justification.

Step 3 — Recommendation:
- For use case '{{use_case}}': Recommend [winner] because [reasons]
- Runner-up: [option] — choose this if [different priorities]
- Avoid: [option] — unless [specific edge case]

Migration considerations:
- Switching cost between options
- Lock-in risk
- Interoperability options

Final verdict: One paragraph with confident recommendation.
